titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick English to Kate English, 12 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick English

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is from a collection Patrick English (1894-1970), who at the time of writing was a prisoner inFrongoch internment camp, recently transferred from Stafford prison. This is part of a letter from Patrick to his mother, Kate English (b. 1855). In the letter, Patrick apologises for not writing before as he is only allowed two letters a week. He goes on to state it would be foolish for his mother the come to visit him and believes it would be a waste of money. Patrick then discusses his health and Father Moore, who he left at Stafford Prison. The remaining pages of the letter are missing.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
No 1637 Hut No 2
Upper Camp
Frongoch
Near Bala
North Wales 12 .7.16 Dear Mother
Sorry I could not write
before this but as I told you in my last
letter we are only allowed to send two
letters every week and as for you com
ing to see me I think it would be a
foolish idea for we would have to
send a notice to the war office first
then we would be only allowed a 15
minutes of a interview so you know
it would only be waste of money
for it would cost you a good deal
to come here but if you wish to
come I wont stop you as regards my
self I am all right nothing to do and
plenty of time to do it. I am glad to see
by your letter you got the post card
quite safe form father Moore but sorry
to say we left him behind us in Stafford
he done a lot of work for us.
